skill.name: azure-ai-document-intelligence-ts
skill.description: Extract text, tables, and structured data from documents using Azure Document Intelligence REST API for TypeScript
skill.summary: TypeScript client for Azure Document Intelligence to analyze invoices, receipts, IDs, and custom documents
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static analysis findings are false positives. The detected 'backtick execution' patterns are markdown code block delimiters, not shell commands. Network URLs point to legitimate Azure endpoints. Environment variable access follows standard credential management practices. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Extract structured data from documents with Azure AI
content.value_statement: Manually processing invoices, receipts, and forms is slow and error-prone. This skill automates document analysis using Azure Document Intelligence to extract text, tables, and fields with high accuracy.
actual_capabilities: Analyze documents using prebuilt models for invoices, receipts, IDs, and tax forms
Extract text, tables, and layout structure from PDFs and images
Build custom document models for specialized document types
Create document classifiers to auto-categorize document types
Process documents from URLs or local files with base64 encoding
Handle long-running async operations with built-in polling
limitations: Requires Azure Document Intelligence subscription and API credentials
Document analysis is asynchronous and requires polling for results
Custom model training requires labeled training data in Azure Blob Storage
API usage is subject to Azure service quotas and pricing tiers

use_cases.title: Accounts Payable Automation
use_cases.description: Automatically extract vendor name, invoice total, and due dates from supplier invoices for accounting workflows
use_cases.target_user: Finance teams processing high volumes of invoices

use_cases.title: Expense Report Processing
use_cases.description: Parse receipt images to extract merchant, items, and totals for expense management systems
use_cases.target_user: HR and finance departments managing employee reimbursements

use_cases.title: Identity Verification
use_cases.description: Extract name, date of birth, and ID numbers from driver licenses and passports for KYC workflows

prompt_templates.title: Basic Document Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this invoice PDF using Azure Document Intelligence. Extract the vendor name, invoice number, total amount, and due date. Return the results in a clear summary.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick extraction of standard invoice fields from a single document

prompt_templates.title: Receipt Item Extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this receipt image and extract all line items with their descriptions and prices. Also extract the merchant name, transaction date, and total amount. List each item separately.
prompt_templates.scenario: Detailed expense tracking requiring itemized receipt data

prompt_templates.title: Batch Document Classification
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a folder of mixed documents (invoices, receipts, and contracts). Use a document classifier to identify each document type, then apply the appropriate prebuilt model to extract relevant fields from each. Summarize the results by document type.
prompt_templates.scenario: Processing heterogeneous document collections with automatic categorization

prompt_templates.title: Custom Form Processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a custom model to extract fields from our company's purchase order forms. The forms have fields for PO number, requester, department, line items with product codes and quantities, and approval signatures. Guide me through training data preparation and model deployment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating specialized extraction for non-standard internal documents

output_examples.output:

Invoice Number: INV-2024-0892
Invoice Total: $1,247.50

Confidence: 98.2%

  - Caffee Latte (Large): $5.45
  - Blueberry Muffin: $3.95
Subtotal: $9.40
Tax: $0.85
Total: $10.25

best_practices: Always use getLongRunningPoller() for document analysis since operations are asynchronous
Check confidence scores on extracted fields and set minimum thresholds for your use case
Use prebuilt models when available before investing in custom model training
anti_patterns: Do not skip error handling with isUnexpected() - always validate API responses
Avoid polling with manual loops - use the built-in poller for long-running operations
Do not hardcode credentials - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault for API keys

faq.question: What document formats are supported?
faq.answer: Azure Document Intelligence supports PDF, JPEG, PNG, BMP, TIFF, and HEIF formats. PDF is recommended for multi-page documents.

faq.question: How long does document analysis take?
faq.answer: Analysis time varies by document complexity and page count. Simple documents take seconds, while complex multi-page documents may take several minutes. The skill handles polling automatically.

faq.question: What is the difference between template and neural custom models?
faq.answer: Template models require documents with consistent layouts. Neural models handle layout variations and are recommended for most use cases, but require more training samples.

faq.question: Can I process documents stored locally?
faq.answer: Yes, local files are supported by encoding them as base64 and sending in the request body. The skill demonstrates this pattern in the local file example.

faq.question: How do I handle low-confidence field extractions?
faq.answer: Each extracted field includes a confidence score. Set a threshold (e.g., 0.8) and flag low-confidence results for human review before using in downstream processes.

faq.question: What Azure region should I use?
faq.answer: Use the endpoint URL from your Azure Document Intelligence resource. Choose a region close to your users for lower latency and to meet data residency requirements.
